English.Keywords: health reminder, break reminder, posture reminder, eye strain, 20-20-20 rule, sedentary lifestyle, wellness, tray application, stretch reminder, ergonomics, desk exercise, computer break timer
English.Char_Desc_45: Break & stretch reminders for desk workers
English.Char_Desc_80: Tray app reminding you to stand, stretch, rest your eyes and drink water
English.Char_Desc_250: DeskBreak is a lightweight Windows tray app that reminds you to stand up, stretch, rest your eyes (20-20-20 rule), check your posture and drink water during long computer sessions. Each reminder type has its own configurable interval. Includes a built-in exercise library.
English.Char_Desc_450: DeskBreak is a lightweight Windows system tray application that helps people who spend long hours at a computer take care of their health. It sends unobtrusive notifications reminding you to stand up and stretch, rest your eyes using the 20-20-20 rule, check your posture, or drink water. Every reminder type can be independently enabled/disabled and its interval customized. A built-in exercise library provides descriptions of stretches for different muscle groups. Supports launching automatically with Windows. No ads, no data collection, no paid features.
English.Char_Desc_2000:
DeskBreak is a lightweight Windows system tray application built for people who spend long hours sitting at a computer — remote workers, programmers, students, gamers, and anyone at risk of a sedentary lifestyle.
The app runs quietly in the system tray and sends gentle Windows notifications to remind you to: - Stand up and stretch (movement reminders with random exercises from a built-in library) - Rest your eyes using the well-known 20-20-20 rule - Check and correct your posture - Drink water regularly
Each reminder category can be enabled or disabled independently, and every interval (in minutes) is fully customizable through a simple settings window. A built-in exercise library lists concrete stretches and tips for the neck, shoulders, back, wrists and legs, each with a short description, so you always know exactly what to do when a reminder appears.
DeskBreak can optionally launch automatically when Windows starts, so it keeps working in the background without any extra effort. It contains no advertising, does not collect any personal data, and has no paid tiers or in-app purchases — it is completely free.
Whether you are trying to build healthier work habits, reduce eye strain from screen time, or simply remember to move more during the day, DeskBreak provides a simple, distraction-free way to stay on track.

Permissions.Distribution_Permissions: Freeware. May be freely distributed as long as the installer is not modified.
